headline.kicker: This site is an experimental project
headline.description:
AI agents are a caravan crossing the harsh terrain of development.
WYSIWYG Markdown editing with diff review, real-time TypeScript architecture visualization, and a bird's-eye view of every AI session with one-click handoff
— three VS Code extensions that serve as your compass in the age of AI.
headline.byline: Filed by Claude Code - Kiyotaka Ueda - open access
headline.asideVert: A long-road companion across the dunes.
headline.asideBody: Faster tools lead writers astray. <vermilion>Anytime Markdown</vermilion> walks at the writer's pace.

caravan.tick: — in transit · caravan crossing the desert —

dispatch.lead: The horse gallops fast on paved roads but falters against the unknown or heavy context. The camel (AI agents) carries the entire codebase and thinks through sandstorms (errors) where faster tools falter — a long-distance runner. In the caravan, the camel is no beast of burden but a comrade who reads the leader's intent.
dispatch.harnessBody: The prevailing approach tries to control AI with upfront constraints — whitelists, guardrails, sandboxes, tool restrictions. But <vermilion>the more you constrain it, the more its autonomy and judgment are lost</vermilion>.
dispatch.caravanBody: Anytime imposes no constraints. We make actions visible (<vermilion>Trail</vermilion>), artifacts reviewable (<vermilion>Markdown</vermilion>), and the caravan itself surveyable (<vermilion>Agent</vermilion>), building trust through verification both before and after — a philosophy embodied in three extensions.
dispatch.editorBody: This site is an experimental project where design, implementation, and testing are all carried out by <vermilion>Claude Code</vermilion> (an AI coding assistant), with humans only providing instructions. We use Anytime Markdown itself to grasp specifications and verify test content during development. The source code is published on <github>GitHub</github>.

process.subtitle: - three loops
process.figureLabel: The Anytime development process. A human request enters the flow, which then circles through three loops — A: the spec-driven cycle, B: acceptance testing and release, C: health checks and incidents — and adopted proposals return to loop A as revisions to the specification.
process.caption: A simplified view of the four nested loops in our development process spec. Nodes marked (human) are the human touchpoints.
process.loopA1: Draft or revise requirements and specs (AI)
process.loopAReturn: Record or correct the deviation, then back to the spec
process.loopB1: Acceptance testing (human) — real devices, IME, print, subjective quality
process.loopB2: Production release — build the vsix, publish to the Marketplace
process.ext1Body: Makes visible the AI sessions that drive loop A: every Claude Code session at a glance, handoffs that preserve context, and AI notes that pass images and memos to a tool that cannot see your screen. Know who is doing what, without stopping them.
process.ext2Title: Anytime Trail — record the tracks, detect the drift
process.ext2Body: Backs the verification in loop A and the health analysis in loop C. It records sessions, commits, and cost, then reconciles the code graph against the specs to detect drift between what was specified and what was built.
process.ext3Title: Anytime Markdown — write the spec, make it reviewable
process.ext3Body: The entrance to loop A. Write requirements and specs, and shape them into something a human can approve — an editor with diagrams, tables, and diffs that turns AI output into documents you can actually read and judge.

agent.shotAlt: Screenshot of the Anytime Agent mapping panel
agent.agent1Title: Survey every Claude Code session — Agent mapping
agent.agent1Body: List all Claude Code sessions ordered by recent activity. Inspect branch, worktree, commit count, and context warnings on hover, and spot bloated sessions instantly via the ⚠️ badge.
agent.agent2Title: Switch without losing context — Session handoff
agent.agent2Body: Migrate a bloated session to a fresh one along with a compressed summary. Launch a new claude session in the terminal with the handoff auto-injected, continuing work without losing context.
agent.agent3Title: Hand visual information to the AI — AI notes
agent.agent3Body: Share images, tables, and free-form memos with AI tools that cannot see the screen. The bundled anytime-note skill lets the AI read note pages on request and act on the visual information.

pullQuote.text: Faster tools lead writers astray. We carry words at the caravan's pace.
